overview: Midjourney V6+ handles BOTH keyword lists and natural language, but natural language now works better for complex prompts. Artist name placement matters critically: 'portrait by [artist]' outperforms '[artist]'s portrait of...' — the model gives more weight to earlier terms. V6 can go ~7 descriptive elements before losing focus. Image weight (--iw) now goes 0.1-3.0. Style references (artist names, art movements) remain the single highest-leverage lever — one strong reference changes everything.

style_reference.description: Artist name, art movement, film stock, or medium. Put it EARLY in the prompt — Midjourney gives more weight to earlier terms. One strong reference outperforms five vague ones.

prompt_structure.description: V6+ responds well to natural language. Word order affects output significantly without a fixed seed. Structure: [subject + key descriptor], [environment/context], [style reference], [lighting], [parameters]. Don't exceed ~7 distinct elements before Midjourney starts dropping details.
best_terms: Naturally described scene with artist reference early in prompt

aspect_ratio.description: Without --ar, Midjourney defaults to 1:1 and composition suffers. Always set it. It changes the entire framing.

lighting.description: Short specific lighting terms. Put near the end after subject and style reference.

parameters.description: V6+ parameters that control output. --iw now ranges 0.1-3.0 (was 0.2-2.0 in V5).

composition.description: Visual arrangement — describe when composition is critical to the concept.

power_patterns: Portrait of [subject description], [setting], [lighting descriptor], by [photographer], shot on [camera/film], --ar 4:5 --s 500
antipatterns: Putting artist name late in the prompt — V6 weights early terms more. Put the reference within the first 5 words.
More than 7 elements — Midjourney V6 starts dropping details after ~7 distinct descriptors. Prioritize ruthlessly.
No --ar — you're leaving composition to chance. Always set it.
Two competing artist styles — they cancel each other out. Pick one strong reference.
Generic color terms ('colorful', 'vibrant') — use specific palette language: 'teal and orange grade', 'warm golden palette', 'desaturated with cyan tones'.
Image prompt + name of person — adding a well-known person's name to their image weakens the influence of the text prompt. Omit the name.

strong: elderly woman in a morning market, Hong Kong, by Vivian Maier, natural window light, candid, shot on Leica M6, Kodak Portra 400, --ar 3:2 --s 600
abandoned Victorian greenhouse, overgrown with vines, dramatic shaft of sunlight through broken glass, by Gregory Crewdson, cold fog, --ar 16:9 --s 750 --chaos 10
weak: A beautiful, amazing, vibrant, colorful, artistic woman standing in a forest with gorgeous dramatic lighting and nice composition.
Cool amazing photo of a city at night, colorful and dramatic and moody and cinematic and beautiful.
